theme_archetype: Hybrid: Luxury (Dark Theme) + Organic & Earthy (Light Theme)

colors.justification: Lifted the base background from a harsh, polarizing near-black (0.11 lightness) to a softer, warmer deep umber/charcoal (0.18 lightness). Boosted the mid-text from 0.68 to 0.84 lightness. This significantly warms up the dark mode, reduces astigmatism-induced 'haloing', and drastically improves reading comfort without losing the moody cellar aesthetic.

theatrical_hero_pattern.strategy: Section-by-Section Theme Scoping with a CSS Gradient Bridge
theatrical_hero_pattern.css_approach:
Do not rewrite the app. Simply wrap your
and #hero section in a div/section with a `.dark` (or `[data-theme='dark']`) class to force the dark CSS variables natively. Immediately following the hero, add a transition block: `
` (using your exact light base hex equivalent). The remaining page content sits in a container explicitly set to light theme. This relies purely on your existing CSS custom property scoping.
theatrical_hero_pattern.benefits: Zero scroll-jacking, no complex JS intersection observers, highly performant, and completely frees users from reading long body copy in dark mode while keeping the 5-second 'brand wow' at the top.

page_by_page_recommendation.justification: Sets the 'brand wow' hook immediately, but drops into the warm, readable parchment for the long explanatory copy.
Maintains marketing consistency. Dark hero establishes authority; light body ensures comprehension.
Dark hero for the value prop, but the actual pricing cards and feature lists should sit in the light section for absolute clarity and conversion optimization.
Same pattern. Establishes premium exclusivity up top, readable details below.
Long-form reading must prioritize ergonomics. Stick to the light parchment theme entirely. Dark mode long-form creates high cognitive load.
Chat involves intensive reading of paragraph-length AI responses. Default to light for cognitive ease (the user specifically complained about this page).
Data-dense interfaces (pipelines, funnels) benefit from dark mode to reduce eye strain. The new, softer revised dark mode provides the necessary contrast without the polarizing pure-black harshness.
Short, highly theatrical, single-screen experience. The user is in and out in 15 seconds. Maximize the 'wow' factor here.
Perfect testbed for the revised dark mode. Data-dense and technical.

industry_standard_references.name: Apple (iPhone Pro Marketing Pages)
industry_standard_references.note: Masters of the 'theatrical dark hero'. The page loads in complete blackness to show off the hardware (the 'wow'), then cross-fades or hard-cuts into white/light gray backgrounds for dense technical spec reading.

industry_standard_references.name: Stripe (Press/Newsroom)
industry_standard_references.note: Uses stark, dramatic dark headers that smoothly transition into highly legible, crisp light-themed article bodies.

industry_standard_references.name: Linear
industry_standard_references.note: The gold standard for a highly readable, non-polarizing dark mode. Their dark mode isn't pure black; it's a soft, elevated dark gray that prevents eye strain—exactly what we are achieving with the revised `--ow-bg-base`.

wine_cellar_heritage_lever.texture: Implement a very subtle SVG noise/grain overlay (opacity: 0.03 to 0.05) exclusively on the dark background surfaces. This breaks the 'digital flatness' and subtly simulates the organic, imperfect texture of aged cork, paper, or a concrete tank.
wine_cellar_heritage_lever.typography: Use the 'Fraunces' serif strictly for H1/H2s, taking advantage of its optical sizing. Use Fraunces italics for specific accents (e.g., italicizing conjunctions like 'and'/'or', or key nouns) to mimic a vintner's handwritten logs.
wine_cellar_heritage_lever.brand_amber: Treat `--ow-amber` as a precious resource. Do not use it for large background blocks. Use it as an accent laser—e.g., a 1px top border on a card, the active state ring of an input, or a single glowing dot indicator. This constraint makes it feel luxurious and 'heritage'.

text_mid_vs_bg_base.contrast_status: Passes WCAG AA (and AAA in most contexts)
text_mid_vs_bg_base.details: The perceived lightness difference between 18% and 84% provides a contrast ratio well above the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. It effectively sits around 8.5:1, offering crystal-clear legibility without the 'vibrating' astigmatism effect of pure white on pure black.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Replace the existing dark-mode variables in `/app/client/src/index.css` with the revised ones provided here. 2. For the marketing pages (`/`, `/pricing`, `/why-ownology`), wrap the hero section in a theme override (e.g., `.dark` class) and the rest of the content in `.light`. 3. Add the CSS gradient transition block between them. 4. Ensure `/free-run` defaults strictly to the light theme.
